name: fin_journals
title: Journals
description: Double-entry transaction headers; the actual debit/credit rows live in fin_journal_lines. Reconciled against a private predecessor-system audit, not part of this repo (its Journal model: '+lines; draft/posted; contact/project/document links; period_locked'). Whether a journal balances (total_debits_cents == total_credits_cents) is a COMPUTED property (see object_finance.journal_totals), never enforced here -- see the status field's help below for why, matching the source's own weak spot exactly (reconciled against a private predecessor-system audit, not part of this repo: 'posting is a bare status flip -- balance NOT enforced at post'). v2 adds generated_from (plan/vocabulary/61-materialize-spec.md, additive): a small optional JSON provenance stamp on a journal materialize generated (definition id, source row id, period), left blank on a hand-created journal.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.help: posted is terminal (no move back to draft) -- matches the predecessor system exactly, which never offered one either. Posting is a bare status flip, owner-gated, and nothing more: it does NOT check total_debits_cents == total_credits_cents first. This is not an oversight -- the source's own posting flow never enforced or even computed a balance at post time (reconciled against a private predecessor-system audit, not part of this repo, whose own words are: 'posting is a bare status flip -- balance NOT enforced at post'). journal_totals()/is_balanced (object_finance.py) is available for a caller (a report, a warning banner) to check before or after posting, but nothing in this schema or package blocks an unbalanced journal from being posted. See dbbasic-package.json's Deferred list -- adding that check would be a real improvement over the source, tracked there as future work, not built in this migration.

fields.name: contact_id
fields.label: Contact
fields.help: Optional cross-reference to a contact/customer/vendor id. Plain text, not an enforced relation -- this package has no hard dependency on app-contacts, same posture as app-invoices' source_order_id and app-orders' product_id-optional pattern.

fields.name: reference
fields.label: Reference
fields.help: Free-text external reference (invoice number, check number, bank reference, etc.).

fields.name: generated_from
fields.label: Generated From
fields.help: Provenance stamp (JSON: definition_id/source_id/period_start) set when this journal was created by a materialize_definitions row (plan/vocabulary/61-materialize-spec.md) rather than hand-entered. Blank for a hand-created journal. An ordinary (not schema read_only) field, same reasoning as app-orders' order_totals-stamped fields: object_materialize's write is a plain field submission through create_collection_record, no special escape hatch -- keeping this field read_only would need one. Omitted from forms.default so the generated form never renders it as an editable input.

fields.name: entity_id
fields.label: Entity
fields.help: The set of books this belongs to (65 multi-entity). Optional during rollout; backfilled to the owner's default entity, set by the entity switcher on new writes.
